steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the text. The text establishes that the sonnet has a reputation for rigidity, then argues (via Carl Phillips) that it actually invites experimentation and variety.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'most modern examples are conventional': The text says the opposite — it highlights experimental sonnets and argues the form invites variety. ✗
  • Choice B — 'encourages variety despite suggestions it wouldn't': Matches exactly: the sonnet's rigid reputation is contrasted with its actual capacity for experimentation. ✓
  • Choice C — 'unexpected that the sonnet remains popular given antiquated features': The text is about variety and experimentation, not about the sonnet's continued popularity. ✗
  • Choice D — 'long period with little innovation before it was recognized': The text makes no historical claim about a period of stagnation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
